fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maine Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maine - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity maine, how to transfer physical therapist license to maine, physical therapist compact maine, maine physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Maine Board of Examiners in Physical Therapy
board.parent_dept: Maine Office of Professional and Occupational Regulation (OPOR)

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Maine.

steps.title: Register on the ALMS Portal
steps.description: Create an account on the Maine Online Professional Licensing Services (ALMS) portal at www.pfr.maine.gov/almsonline. This is required for all new applicants.

steps.title: Submit Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Complete the online application for 'Physical Therapist Licensure by Endorsement' (or examination, if applicable) via the ALMS portal and pay the non-refundable $21.00 application fee.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your accredited physical therapy program to the Maine Board. Request license verification from all states where you hold or have held a PT license to be sent directly to the Board. Request a score transfer of your NPTE scores from FSBPT to the Maine Board.

steps.title: Complete the Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Take and pass the Maine Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Exam, which is typically accessible online as part of the ALMS application process.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Records Check (CHRC)
steps.description: After submitting your application, you will receive an email with instructions to complete a fingerprint-based Criminal History Records Check (CHRC) through the IdentoGO vendor.

steps.title: Pay Initial Licensure Fee
steps.description: Upon approval of your application, you will be notified to pay the initial $25.00 licensure fee through the ALMS portal to activate your Maine PT license.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Maine physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Maine have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Maine participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Maine without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Maine?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maine while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Maine
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Maine, you must register and submit your application through the Maine Online Professional Licensing Services (ALMS) portal.

official_links.registration_steps: First-time users must click 'Register' on the ALMS portal to create an account before logging in to start an application.
